Installation","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":"Problems installing or compiling NumPy"}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":79,"created_at":"2020-11-16T09:05:06Z","updated_at":"2024-10-24T09:44:28Z","closed_at":"2021-11-11T16:31:21Z","author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Reproducing code example:

Install Pandas with PIP after a clean install of macOS Big Sur and Python3.9 via Homebrew.

```python
pip install pandas
pip3 install pandas
pip3 install pandas --no-build-isolation --no-cache-dir
sudo pip3 install pandas --no-cache-dir
```

These are the only packages actually installed:
```
Package    Version
---------- -------
pip        20.2.4
setuptools 50.3.2
wheel      0.35.1
```
### Error message:

### NumPy/Python version information:

I have also installed NumPy first (numpy 1.19.4) still getting the same error.
I have opened the following issues and in both of them they said that it's a Numpy error: [Pandas#37880](https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas/issues/37880) and [Pip#9138](https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/9138)

If you are using homebrew, replace Mac OS BLAS by openblas for pip install.
> 
> `brew install openblas` `OPENBLAS="$(brew --prefix openblas)" pip install numpy pandas`

This worked on Mac M1 Pro as well. Thank you so much.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2434798894/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
